summary: This cluster examines the phylogeographic history and biogeographic distribution of the red macroalga *Callophyllis variegata*, focusing on its amphitropical presence between hemispheres. Using haplotype data and biogeographic analyses, the research traced the species’ origin to a refugial population during the late Pleistocene. Key findings indicate that *C. variegata* was likely transported across tropical regions via rafting, representing a rare case of non-native amphitropical distribution. The geographic scope centers on Chilean populations and their connection to other temperate regions, highlighting long-distance dispersal mechanisms in macroalgae.
